question.label: Confidence trigger
question.name: What tends to make you pause before shipping a ticket?
question.description: Surfaces the concrete trigger behind late reassurance-seeking.

answer: Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.

assessment.note: Names missing pairing as the thing that would prevent getting stuck silently, which signals current friction and support gaps; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] pairing once or twice a week is a concrete support thread to pick up next session.

new_queue.label: Support cadence
new_queue.name: What kind of check-in from me would be most useful over the next two weeks?

new_queue.label: Two-week takeaway
new_queue.name: What do you want to focus on most over the next two weeks?

history.answer_excerpt: Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
I still feel fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
